response:
D


The life cycle of an apple tree (an angiosperm) follows a standard plant progression: seed → germination/seedling → mature tree → flowering → pollination/fertilization → fruit development (containing new seeds) → repetition. Option D directly matches a key transition in this sequence, as the ovary of a fertilized flower develops into the apple fruit. 

Options A and C are invalid, as they describe animal (e.g., insect) or incorrect plant processes with no relevance to trees. Option B reverses the actual order, as flowers precede and develop into fruit (not vice versa).
